heading: Private Endpoints for Dedicated Clusters
body:
**AWS PrivateLink:**AWS PrivateLinkMongoDB Atlas supports private endpoints on AWS (Amazon Web Services) using the
[AWS PrivateLink](https://aws.amazon.com/privatelink/) feature.For [dedicated clusters](manual/reference/glossary), Atlas
charges you based on your usage across the following dimensions:* Atlas [Private Endpoint](privatelink-billing-endpoint): The sum of the hourly costs
  incurred to host private endpoints in all AWS (Amazon Web Services) regions.

* Atlas [Private Endpoint Capacity Units](privatelink-billing-cu): The sum of the hourly costs incurred
  to process traffic to private endpoints.In addition to the costs Atlas charges you, AWS (Amazon Web Services) charges you
for each VPC (Virtual Private Cloud) interface endpoint you create. To ensure
cluster availability in the event of a widespread network
outage, create VPC (Virtual Private Cloud) interface endpoints in at least two
availability zones per region. AWS (Amazon Web Services) also charges you for the data
volume each VPC (Virtual Private Cloud) interface endpoint processes.> **See Also:** [AWS PrivateLink Pricing](https://aws.amazon.com/privatelink/pricing/).
